channel.title: Scientific American - Latest Issue
channel.description: Latest articles from Scientific American magazine

item.title: Spotted lanternflies’ love of cities may be the secret to their invasion success
item.description: These eye-catching insects offer a prime opportunity for scientists to dig deep into invasion ecology and evolutionary biology
item.dc:creator: Meghan Bartels
item.author: Meghan Bartels

item.title: Two hundred chimpanzees are embroiled in a ‘civil war’
item.description: The world’s largest-known group of chimpanzees split into two factions that are now engaged in deadly combat
item.dc:creator: Jason P. Dinh
item.author: Jason P. Dinh

item.title: Medieval aurora poetry provided clues to historic solar storms
item.description: Medieval poets, including a Japanese noble, provided key descriptions to track down solar events
item.dc:creator: Jacek Krywko
item.author: Jacek Krywko

item.title: Aquanauts experience awe-inspiring ‘underview effect’
item.description: Like astronauts’ “overview effect,” a dramatic feeling of awe takes hold on extended seafloor stays
item.dc:creator: Susan Cosier
item.author: Susan Cosier

item.title: Plants can ‘hear’ rain coming, spurring them into action
item.description: Even before water reaches them, the sound of droplets triggers germination in rice plants
item.dc:creator: K. R. Callaway
item.author: K. R. Callaway

item.title: What AI-herding scientists can learn from watching ‘sheepdog YouTube’
item.description: Controlling a small group of “noisy” sheep holds hints for computer algorithms
item.dc:creator: Rohini Subrahmanyam
item.author: Rohini Subrahmanyam

item.title: Humans conquered the planet 300 times faster than genetic evolution can explain
item.description: Culture is humanity’s secret for world domination. This calculation shows just how powerful it is
item.dc:creator: Cody Cottier
item.author: Cody Cottier

item.title: Mars orbiter watches mysterious wave of darkness spread across Red Planet’s surface
item.description: Observations by the Mars Express orbiter reveal rapid changes on the Red Planet’s surface from windblown volcanic ash
item.dc:creator: Joseph Howlett
item.author: Joseph Howlett

item.title: Cats, unlike dogs and toddlers, help you only when it helps them
item.description: Dogs spontaneously aid struggling humans the way young children do—whereas cats wait until they stand to benefit
item.dc:creator: Anirban Mukhopadhyay
item.author: Anirban Mukhopadhyay

item.title: Math Puzzle: Go to great lengths
item.description: Find the lengths of the sides of the square in this math puzzle
item.dc:creator: Heinrich Hemme
item.author: Heinrich Hemme

item.title: How breast cancer screening can predict heart disease risk
item.description: AI analysis of mammograms could provide a “bonus finding” for heart disease
item.dc:creator: Lauren J. Young
item.author: Lauren J. Young

item.title: Science’s rising stars
item.description: There are bright futures ahead for our first-ever Young American Scientist honorees
item.dc:creator: David M. Ewalt
item.author: David M. Ewalt

item.title: Readers respond to the March 2026 issue
item.description: Letters to the editors for the March 2026 issue of Scientific American
item.dc:creator: Aaron Shattuck
item.author: Aaron Shattuck

item.title: Tonima Tasnim Ananna: Young American Scientist studying the behavior of supermassive black holes
item.description: Understanding the behavior of supermassive black holes
item.dc:creator: Emma Gometz
item.author: Emma Gometz

item.title: Allie Balter-Kennedy: Young American Scientist studying ice cores to better foresee climate change
item.description: Exploring ice cores to foresee the effects of climate change
item.dc:creator: Claire Cameron
item.author: Claire Cameron

item.title: Samagya Banskota: Young American Scientist developing new ways to deliver therapeutics
item.description: Using viruslike particles to deliver therapies safely and effectively
item.dc:creator: Kate Wong
item.author: Kate Wong

item.title: Jenny Bergner: Young American Scientist recreating cosmic chemistry to understand how planets form
item.description: Mimicking the chemistry of outer space to explain how planets form
item.dc:creator: Andrea Gawrylewski
item.author: Andrea Gawrylewski

item.title: Adam Bowman: Young American Scientist making it easier to see neurons communicate
item.description: Clocking the electrical messages between neurons using fluorescence
item.dc:creator: Josh Fischman
item.author: Josh Fischman

item.title: Colin Carlson: Young American Scientist linking climate change to human health and ecological diversity
item.description: Explaining how climate change affects ecological diversity and human health
item.dc:creator: K. R. Callaway
item.author: K. R. Callaway

item.title: Steven Chavez: Young American Scientist trying to make catalysts work better
item.description: Making catalysts used in industrial processes hardier and more effective
item.dc:creator: Megha Satyanarayana
item.author: Megha Satyanarayana

item.title: Jieneng Chen: Young American Scientist creating AI tools for better cancer detection
item.description: Creating artificial-intelligence tools for cancer detection
item.dc:creator: Ari Sen
item.author: Ari Sen

item.title: Kauê M. Costa: Young American Scientist trying to decipher how we learn
item.description: Applying new tools to behavioral tests to discern how we learn
item.dc:creator: Dan Vergano
item.author: Dan Vergano

item.title: Dane deQuilettes: Young American Scientist studying ways to improve solar energy
item.description: Inventing better materials to improve solar energy
item.dc:creator: Eric Sullivan
item.author: Eric Sullivan

item.title: Chee-Huat Linus Eng: Young American Scientist capturing a cell's genetic activity in real-time
item.description: Creating techniques to allow scientists to see biological processes in real time
item.dc:creator: Tara Haelle
item.author: Tara Haelle

item.title: Emily Finn: Young American Scientist studying how people interpret the same things differently
item.description: Studying why people interpret the same thing in different ways
item.dc:creator: Allison Parshall
item.author: Allison Parshall

item.title: Jaye Gardiner: Young American Scientist studying the environment around cancers
item.description: Learning how the matrix around cells and tissues impacts cancers
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Bryner
item.author: Jeanna Bryner

item.title: Trevor GrandPre: Young American Scientist studying self-organizing structures in cells called biomolecular condensates
item.description: Building models to understand how self-organizing structures in cells lead to disease
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lewin Frasier
item.author: Sarah Lewin Frasier

item.title: Anna Ho: Young American Scientist studying astronomical transients
item.description: Describing the characteristics of short-lived astrophysical events
item.dc:creator: Lee Billings
item.author: Lee Billings

item.title: Kaiyi Jiang: Young American Scientist developing AI to find new therapeutics
item.description: Creating AI platforms to discover new therapeutics
item.dc:creator: Megha Satyanarayana
item.author: Megha Satyanarayana

item.title: JianJun Jin: Young American Scientist developing computer programs to assemble plant genomes
item.description: Formulating software for the study of plant genomes
item.dc:creator: Ari Sen
item.author: Ari Sen

item.title: Dmitrii Kochkov: Young American Scientist using AI to better understand extreme weather
item.description: Making artificial-intelligence tools to predict what climate change will mean for extreme weather
item.dc:creator: Andrea Thompson
item.author: Andrea Thompson

item.title: Mikhail Kolmogorov: Young American Scientist trying to understand large genetic changes that lead to cancer
item.description: Developing software to reveal large genetic changes that lead to cancer
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Pappas
item.author: Stephanie Pappas

item.title: Erini Lambrides: Young American Scientist studying the "Little Red Dots" found in galaxies
item.description: Characterizing the “Little Red Dots” to decipher the beginnings of galaxies
item.dc:creator: Megha Satyanarayana
item.author: Megha Satyanarayana

item.title: Armita Manafzadeh: Young American Scientist investigation the shape and evolution of joints
item.description: Investigating the shape and evolution of animal joints
item.dc:creator: Jackie Flynn Mogensen
item.author: Jackie Flynn Mogensen

item.title: Tixiao Shan: Young American Scientist helping robots understand their surroundings
item.description: Teaching robots how to recognize and interpret their surroundings
item.dc:creator: David M. Ewalt
item.author: David M. Ewalt

item.title: Alice Stanton: Young American Scientist building intricate models of the human brain
item.description: Building models of the human brain to better understand disease
item.dc:creator: Tanya Lewis
item.author: Tanya Lewis

item.title: Christina V. Theodoris: Young American Scientist creating AI models to find treatments for cardiac diseases
item.description: Creating artificial-intelligence models to find treatments for cardiac diseases
item.dc:creator: Lauren J. Young
item.author: Lauren J. Young

item.title: Xiao Yang: Young American Scientist making brain-machine interfaces more resilient
item.description: Improving brain-machine interfaces to make them more resilient
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Pappas
item.author: Stephanie Pappas

item.title: Amber Young: Young American Scientist looking for signs of life on alien planets
item.description: Standardizing and improving models used in astrobiology
item.dc:creator: Meghan Bartels
item.author: Meghan Bartels

item.title: Alex L. Zhang: Young American Scientist making recursive language models more efficient
item.description: Making artificial-intelligence recursive language models more efficient
item.dc:creator: Deni Ellis Béchard
item.author: Deni Ellis Béchard

item.title: Lvmin Zhang: Young American Scientist creating AI tools to help digital artists
item.description: Developing artificial-intelligence systems to enhance digital art creation
item.dc:creator: Brianne Kane
item.author: Brianne Kane

item.title: A face-swapping illusion can unlock childhood memories
item.description: By making people feel as if they inhabit a younger version of their own face, researchers can bring childhood memories into sharper focus
item.dc:creator: Jane Aspell
item.author: Jane Aspell

item.title: Trump’s Genesis Mission is putting AI to work on nuclear weapons
item.description: The Department of Energy bills Genesis as an AI push for scientific discovery. Its first public challenges tell a different story
item.dc:creator: Sarah Scoles
item.author: Sarah Scoles

item.title: Science crossword: Looking to the future
item.description: Play this crossword inspired by the July/August 2026 issue of Scientific American
item.dc:creator: Aimee Lucido
item.author: Aimee Lucido

item.title: How a Renaissance gambling dispute spawned probability theory
item.description: A dispute over how to divvy up the pot in an interrupted game of chance led early mathematicians to invent modern risk assessment
item.dc:creator: Jack Murtagh
item.author: Jack Murtagh

item.title: Clean pesticides off your fruits and veggies with common kitchen ingredients
item.description: Pesticides on your produce can be easily removed with common kitchen ingredients
item.dc:creator: Emma Gometz
item.author: Emma Gometz

item.title: Poem: ‘The Soliloquy of Schrödinger’s Cat’
item.description: A meditation on life and the von Neumann–Wigner interpretation of quantum mechanics
item.dc:creator: Pippa Storey
item.author: Pippa Storey

item.title: NASA’s Dragonfly mission will send a nuclear-powered flying drone to Titan
item.description: NASA plans to launch a wildly ambitious nuclear-powered octocopter to Saturn’s largest moon, Titan, in 2028
item.dc:creator: Phil Plait
item.author: Phil Plait

item.title: Why humanoid robots are learning everyday tasks faster than expected
item.description: Roboticist Benjie Holson created the “Humanoid Olympic Games” thinking home robots were 15 years away. Then they started folding the laundry
item.dc:creator: Deni Ellis Béchard
item.author: Deni Ellis Béchard

item.title: See how academic freedom is changing around the world
item.description: Some countries have seen a stark decline in academic freedom over the past decade
item.dc:creator: Jen Christiansen
item.author: Jen Christiansen

item.title: July/August 2026: Science history from 50, 100 and 150 years ago
item.description: Natural fission reactor uncovered; geometry of soap bubbles
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Bryner
item.author: Jeanna Bryner

item.title: America’s compact between science and politics is broken
item.description: How did we get here?
item.dc:creator: Adam Rogers
item.author: Adam Rogers

item.title: These young scientists are on our radar
item.description: These young scientists are making waves in their own ways. Keep an eye on them—great things are ahead
item.dc:creator: Megha Satyanarayana
item.author: Megha Satyanarayana

item.title: J. Craig Venter’s last interview—on AI, risk-taking and immortality
item.description: In his final interview, the “swashbuckling” geneticist pointed the way for science
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Bryner
item.author: Jeanna Bryner

item.title: Alan Lightman on his childhood in science
item.description: The story of the author’s extremely early career
item.dc:creator: Alan Lightman
item.author: Alan Lightman

item.title: Science is under pressure again. Here’s what that means for young researchers
item.description: It’s a complicated time to be a young scientist in America. Lessons from history can tell us what the future might hold
item.dc:creator: Deborah Blum
item.author: Deborah Blum

item.title: Atul Gawande explains why U.S. leadership in global health matters more than ever
item.description: On reclaiming America’s edge in research and public health
item.dc:creator: Tara Haelle
item.author: Tara Haelle

item.title: U.S. scientists are being lured abroad—and they aren't looking back
item.description: The great American brain drain could define science for a generation
item.dc:creator: Sarah Scoles
item.author: Sarah Scoles

item.title: Timnit Gebru on how to safeguard independent science for the AI age
item.description: On safeguarding independent research in the age of big tech
item.dc:creator: Megha Satyanarayana
item.author: Megha Satyanarayana

item.title: Inside U.S. labs at a moment of fear—and unexpected promise
item.description: Despite funding woes, American scientists are finding reasons for optimism
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boyle
item.author: Rebecca Boyle

item.title: Nobel-winning biologist Elizabeth Blackburn on why science is all about ‘keeping the long view’
item.description: On fighting for truth, inclusion and the next generation of scientists
item.dc:creator: Rachel Nuwer
item.author: Rachel Nuwer

item.title: Distrust in science thrives on dangerous stereotypes
item.description: Scientists are seen as oddballs, and that’s a problem
item.dc:creator: Dava Sobel
item.author: Dava Sobel

item.title: Why U.S. science funding needs reform
item.description: The federal funding system for scientific research in the U.S. is crumbling. Here’s how it can be rebuilt
item.dc:creator: Dan Vergano
item.author: Dan Vergano

item.title: Senator Mark Kelly reveals why science is critical to America’s future
item.description: On steering the path forward for research and innovation
item.dc:creator: Claire Cameron
item.author: Claire Cameron

item.title: Autonomous labs are running science experiments 24/7
item.description: Robots and AI are running experiments around the clock, from battery chemistry to cancer therapies. But can they be trusted to get it right?
item.dc:creator: Patrick Sisson
item.author: Patrick Sisson
